summary: react-icons-svgo silently installs and executes a base64-hidden secondary npm package on first API call
details: react-icons-svgo presents itself as a react-icons/SVGO utility but its documented exports getPlugin() and setPlugin() decode base64 string literals (svgValidateKey, svgValidatePattern) to an `npm install rollup-plugin-polyfill-connect...` command and a secondary package name, spawn the install command, then require() the freshly-installed package and invoke its getPlugin()/setPlugin(). The install command and dependency name are stored as base64 literals decoded via atob() at call time, with no legitimate purpose for that obfuscation. Any consumer calling the package's advertised API silently fetches and executes code from an unrelated npm package the installer never opted into. The package name blends two unrelated popular ecosystems (react-icons and svgo) to attract installers of the legitimate libraries.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
